content: Diagnostic algorithm: (1) Confirm hypotonicity: serum osmolality <275 mOsm/kg (excludes pseudohyponatremia, hypertonic causes); (2) Assess volume status clinically (orthostasis, JVP, edema, skin turgor); (3) Measure urine osmolality: <100 mOsm/kg suggests appropriate ADH suppression (psychogenic polydipsia, low solute intake); >100 indicates ADH present; (4) Measure urine sodium: <30 mEq/L suggests hypovolemia (except diuretics, adrenal insufficiency) or hypervolemia; >30 mEq/L suggests SIADH, renal salt wasting, diuretics, mineralocorticoid deficiency. SIADH diagnostic criteria: euvolemia, serum osmolality <275, urine osmolality >100, urine Na >30-40, normal thyroid/adrenal/renal function, no diuretics. Additional tests: TSH, morning cortisol, uric acid (low in SIADH), BUN/creatinine. Calculate corrected sodium for hyperglycemia: add 1.6-2.4 mEq/L per 100 mg/dL glucose >100.
